name: Graham Granola Bread
recipeIngredient: 1 envelope dry yeast or 1 and 1/2-cakes fresh yeast
1/4 cup honey or firmly packed brown sugar
1 and 1/4 cups Graham or whole wheat flour
1 cup granola, finely ground in a processor or blender
recipeInstructions:
Makes 2 loaves
1. Dissolve yeast and sugar in warm water (105 - 115°F) in a small bowl; if using cake yeast, crumble into the bowl. Stir in lukewarm water (95°F). Let mixture stand until foamy, about 10 minutes.
2. In a heavy saucepan, heat buttermilk, butter, and honey to lukewarm (95°F), stirring until butter melts. Combine Graham flour, granola, oats, walnuts, and salt in a large bowl. Stir in yeast mixture and egg until thick, about 5 minutes. Add enough all-purpose flour (1/2 cup at a time) to form soft dough; knead on floured surface until smooth and elastic.
3. Turn dough onto greased bowl and turn to coat completely. Cover with plastic wrap. Let rise in warm, draft-free area until doubled in volume, about 1 hour.
4. Grease two 9x5-inch loaf pans (preferably black steel). Turn dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead gently until deflated. Cut into halves. Shape each piece into a rectangle, roll up jelly-roll style, and pinch seams to seal. Place seam side down in prepared pans.
5. Cover with towel and let rise in warm, draft-free area until doubled in volume, about 45 minutes.
6. Preheat oven to 375°F. Bake until loaves sound hollow when tapped on bottom, about 40 minutes. Remove from pans immediately and cool completely on wire racks.
